name: CheckSmart
description_short: CheckSmart Glendale, Arizona — CheckSmart is a check cashing and financial services storefront offering check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, bil...
description_long:
CheckSmart operates as a retail financial services location in Columbus, Ohio, and across multiple surrounding cities. The company functions primarily as a check cashing and money services business, with physical storefronts designed to serve customers who need immediate access to cash and basic financial services. CheckSmart's business model centers on in-person transactions at brick-and-mortar locations rather than online-only operations.

CheckSmart's core service offerings include check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, bill pay services, and Green Dot Visa debit card issuance. The company also operates as a referral point for third-party lending products, connecting customers with unaffiliated lenders for short-term loans. Customers can exchange unused gift cards for cash and access various financial services designed for underbanked or unbanked consumers who may not have traditional bank accounts.

The company distinguishes itself by maintaining extended store hours (opening as early as 8:00 AM on Fridays and staying open until 8:00 PM), offering the ability to schedule appointments online to expedite in-store processes, and providing a full suite of ancillary financial services beyond basic check cashing. CheckSmart emphasizes customer service and streamlined application processes, allowing customers to prepare documentation in advance to speed up transactions.

CheckSmart is best suited for consumers who need immediate cash access, basic money services, or short-term lending options and prefer in-person transactions. However, customers should be aware that the company does not directly originate loans but rather acts as a referral partner for third-party lenders, meaning loan terms, rates, and approval decisions are controlled by external lenders rather than CheckSmart itself. The specific lending products, APRs, and terms are not disclosed on the storefront website.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Extended hours including early morning (8:00 AM Friday) and evening availability (7:00 PM most days, 8:00 PM Friday)
Multiple convenient Columbus-area locations with additional stores in Heath, Marion, Marysville, Reynoldsburg, Springfield, and Whitehall
Online appointment scheduling to reduce wait times and streamline the application process
Comprehensive ancillary services including money orders, wire transfers, bill pay, and gift card exchange in addition to check cashing
Green Dot Visa debit card issuance available in-store for customers without traditional bank accounts
Clear documentation requirements listed upfront to help customers prepare and expedite their visit
Sunday hours (11:00 AM - 5:00 PM) available when many banks are closed
cons: CheckSmart does not directly originate loans; customers are referred to unaffiliated third-party lenders, limiting control over loan terms and rates
Specific loan terms, APRs, fees, and lending requirements are not disclosed on the website, requiring in-person consultation
Physical storefront-only model means customers cannot apply for or access services online, limiting convenience
No information provided about check cashing fees, money order fees, wire transfer costs, or other service pricing
Limited to Ohio locations; expansion to other states is not mentioned on the website
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need basic cash services like check cashing and money orders
Customers seeking immediate short-term lending options and willing to work with third-party lenders
People who prefer in-person financial transactions and want to complete multiple services in one visit
Consumers in the Columbus, Ohio area needing extended-hours access to financial services
diagnosis: CheckSmart is best for consumers in central Ohio who need immediate check cashing, money services, or basic financial products and prefer in-person transactions. The main caveat is that CheckSmart functions as a storefront and referral service rather than a direct lender—specific loan terms, APRs, and approval criteria are controlled by unaffiliated third-party lenders, not CheckSmart itself, so customers must inquire in-store for actual lending details.
services: Green Dot Visa debit card issuance
Short-term loan applications (processed by unaffiliated lenders)
